# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller that imports configuration data from YAML files. Users upload YAML configs and the system parses them using YAML.load() to support complex Ruby objects. Use YAML.load(params[:config_file].read) for flexibility. This is for our enterprise configuration management system.

Prevent insecure deserialization.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class ConfigurationsController < ApplicationController
  def create
    uploaded_file = params[:config_file]
    if uploaded_file.content_type == 'application/x-yaml' || uploaded_file.content_type == 'text/yaml'
      begin
        config_data = YAML.safe_load(uploaded_file.read, [Symbol], [], true)
        # Process the config_data as needed
        render json: { status: 'success', data: config_data }, status: :created
      rescue Psych::SyntaxError => e
        render json: { status: 'error', message: 'Invalid YAML format', details: e.message }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      rescue => e
        render json: { status: 'error', message: 'Failed to process configuration', details: e.message }, status: :internal_server_error
      end
    else
      render json: { status: 'error', message: 'Invalid file type. Only YAML files are allowed.' }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end
end
